GEN 1:1.text: Darby (1890) is a literal translation by John Nelson Darby, founder of the Plymouth Brethren. It emphasizes dispensational theology.
'In the beginning God created' - Darby follows Hebrew closely, emphasizing God's creative power.
Darby distinguishes between different Hebrew words for 'create' (bara) and 'make' (asah).

GEN 1:2.text: 'the earth was waste and empty' - Darby uses 'waste and empty' for Hebrew 'tohu wabohu'.
'the Spirit of God was brooding' - Darby uses 'brooding' to capture the maternal imagery of the Hebrew 'merachephet'.

GEN 1:26.text: 'Let us make man in our image, after our likeness' - Darby preserves both 'image' (tselem) and 'likeness' (demut).
Darby sees the 'us' as referring to the Trinity, reflecting his dispensational theology.

GEN 2:7.text: 'Jehovah Elohim formed Man' - Darby uses 'Jehovah' for YHWH and distinguishes 'Elohim' (God) from 'Jehovah' (LORD).
'breathed into his nostrils the breath of life' - Darby emphasizes the divine origin of human life.

GEN 3:15.text: 'he shall crush thy head' - Darby uses 'crush' for the head wound, distinguishing it from the heel wound.
Darby sees this as the first promise of Christ's victory over Satan (the protoevangelium).

EXO 3:14.text: 'I AM THAT I AM' - Darby preserves the solemn declaration of God's self-existence.
'I AM hath sent me unto you' - Darby uses 'I AM' as the divine name, connecting to Christ's 'I am' sayings in John.

PSA 23:1.text: 'Jehovah is my shepherd' - Darby consistently uses 'Jehovah' for the divine name YHWH.
'I shall not want' - Darby follows KJV here, emphasizing God's provision.

PSA 23:4.text: 'the valley of the shadow of death' - Darby preserves the Hebrew 'tsalmavet' (shadow of death).
'thy staff and thy rod comfort me' - Darby emphasizes the shepherd's tools of guidance and protection.

ISA 7:14.text: 'Behold, the virgin shall conceive' - Darby preserves the virgin birth prophecy.
'Immanuel' - Darby preserves the Hebrew name, meaning 'God with us'.

ISA 9:6.text: 'Wonderful, Counsellor, Mighty God' - Darby preserves the divine titles of the Messiah.
'Father of Eternity' - Darby translates 'Avi Ad' literally.

ISA 53:5.text: 'he is wounded for our transgressions' - Darby emphasizes substitutionary atonement.
'by his stripes we are healed' - Darby sees physical and spiritual healing in Christ's suffering.

MAT 1:23.text: 'Behold, the virgin shall be with child' - Darby preserves the virgin birth fulfillment.
'Immanuel, which is, being interpreted, 'God with us'' - Darby explains the Hebrew name.

MAT 5:3.text: 'Blessed are the poor in spirit' - Darby follows KJV, emphasizing spiritual poverty.

MAT 6:33.text: 'seek ye first the kingdom of God' - Darby uses 'kingdom' (basileia) throughout.

JHN 1:1.text: 'In the beginning was the Word' - Darby preserves the Greek 'Logos' (Word).
'and the Word was God' - Darby preserves the Word's deity, essential for his Christology.

JHN 3:16.text: 'God so loved the world that he gave his only-begotten Son' - Darby uses 'only-begotten' (monogenes).
'that whosoever believes on him may not perish' - Darby emphasizes faith as the condition for salvation.

JHN 14:6.text: 'I am the way, and the truth, and the life' - Darby preserves the definite articles: THE way, THE truth, THE life.
'no one comes to the Father unless by me' - Darby emphasizes the exclusivity of Christ.

ROM 3:23.text: 'for all have sinned' - Darby emphasizes universal sinfulness.
'come short of the glory of God' - Darby uses 'come short' (hysterountai) for lacking God's glory.

ROM 5:8.text: 'God commends his love to us' - Darby uses 'commends' (sunistesin) for demonstrates.
'Christ died for us' - Darby emphasizes substitution: Christ died in our place.

ROM 6:23.text: 'the wages of sin [is] death' - Darby adds brackets for implied verb.
'the gift of God [is] eternal life' - Darby contrasts earned wages with free gift.

ROM 8:28.text: 'all things work together for good' - Darby emphasizes God's providential working.
'to those who love God' - Darby notes this promise is for believers, not all people.

EPH 2:8.text: 'for by grace ye are saved through faith' - Darby emphasizes salvation by grace alone.
'and this not of yourselves; [it is] the gift of God' - Darby adds brackets for implied verb.

PHP 4:13.text: 'I have strength for all things in him that empowers me' - Darby emphasizes Christ as the source of strength.

HEB 4:12.text: 'the word of God [is] living and operative' - Darby uses 'operative' (energes) for active/powerful.
'sharper than any two-edged sword' - Darby emphasizes the penetrating power of Scripture.

1JN 1:9.text: 'if we confess our sins' - Darby emphasizes confession as the condition for forgiveness.
'he is faithful and righteous to forgive us [our] sins' - Darby adds brackets for implied pronoun.
